questions.q: Who directed the 1973-74 Hindi film Garm Hava?
questions.explanation: Garm Hava was directed by M. S. Sathyu and was his feature-film directorial debut.

questions.q: The film Garm Hava is centred on a family from which religious community deciding whether to remain in India after Partition?
questions.explanation: Garm Hava follows a north Indian Muslim family weighing whether to stay in India or migrate to Pakistan after the 1947 Partition.

questions.q: In which Indian city is the story of Garm Hava primarily set?
questions.explanation: Garm Hava is set in Agra, where the Mirza family runs a shoe-manufacturing business.

questions.q: Garm Hava is set against the backdrop of which major event in Indian history?
questions.explanation: The film dramatises the aftermath of the 1947 Partition of India and its impact on a Muslim family that chooses to stay.

questions.q: Which veteran actor played the lead role of Salim Mirza in Garm Hava?
questions.explanation: Balraj Sahni played the patriarch Salim Mirza in Garm Hava, in one of the final and most celebrated roles of his career.

questions.q: Garm Hava was adapted from an unpublished short story by which Urdu writer?
questions.explanation: The film is based on an unpublished short story by the noted Urdu writer Ismat Chughtai.

questions.q: What is the family business of the Mirzas in Garm Hava?
questions.explanation: Salim Mirza in Garm Hava runs a traditional shoe-manufacturing business that suffers as Partition disrupts his world.

questions.q: In which decade was Garm Hava first released?
questions.explanation: Garm Hava was completed in 1973 and released in 1974, in the 1970s.

questions.q: Which renowned poet and lyricist co-wrote the screenplay and dialogue of Garm Hava?
questions.explanation: The poet Kaifi Azmi co-wrote the screenplay and dialogue of Garm Hava and also wrote its lyrics.

questions.q: Garm Hava was primarily made in which language?
questions.explanation: Garm Hava is a Hindi-Urdu language film, with dialogue steeped in the Urdu idiom of north India.

questions.q: Garm Hava ran into trouble with which body, which initially withheld its release for several months?
options: The Central Board of Film Censors
questions.explanation: The Censor Board initially held up Garm Hava over fears it could provoke communal tension before clearing it for release.

questions.q: Garm Hava won a National Film Award in the category recognising work on which theme?
questions.explanation: Garm Hava received the National Film Award for Best Feature Film on National Integration (the Nargis Dutt Award).

questions.q: Shaukat Kaifi (Shaukat Azmi), wife of co-writer Kaifi Azmi, acted in Garm Hava. Which character did she play?
options: Jamila, the wife of Salim Mirza
questions.explanation: Shaukat Kaifi played Jamila, the steadfast wife of patriarch Salim Mirza, in Garm Hava.

questions.q: Apart from his film work, Kaifi Azmi, the co-writer of Garm Hava, was a celebrated figure in which field?
questions.explanation: Kaifi Azmi, who co-wrote Garm Hava, was one of the most celebrated Urdu poets of the 20th century.

questions.q: Garm Hava is widely regarded as one of the early landmarks of which movement in Indian film?
questions.explanation: Garm Hava is considered a foundational work of Indian parallel cinema, also called the New Indian Cinema.

questions.q: Which writer collaborated with Kaifi Azmi on the script and dialogue of Garm Hava and is credited for the screenplay?
questions.explanation: Shama Zaidi worked with Kaifi Azmi on the screenplay and dialogue of Garm Hava.

questions.q: A recurring symbol in Garm Hava is a form of transport that the family is repeatedly seen riding through Agra. What is it?
questions.explanation: The tonga and its driver recur through Garm Hava, framing the family's journeys and the film's reflective tone.

questions.q: Garm Hava was India's official entry in the foreign-language category at the Academy Awards for which year's films?
questions.explanation: Garm Hava was selected as India's official submission for the Best Foreign Language Film Oscar for 1974.

questions.q: Garm Hava received the Filmfare Critics Award for Best Movie. In which awards body's critics' category was it honoured?
questions.explanation: Garm Hava won the Filmfare Critics Award for Best Movie.

questions.q: Balraj Sahni, the lead actor of Garm Hava, died at what point relative to the film's completion?
options: Shortly after completing his work on it
questions.explanation: Balraj Sahni died in 1973, shortly after finishing his work on Garm Hava, making it one of his last films.

questions.q: What was M. S. Sathyu's primary background before directing Garm Hava?
questions.explanation: M. S. Sathyu came from a background in theatre and art direction, including work with IPTA, before directing Garm Hava.

questions.q: Garm Hava was made on a notably modest budget. How is the film generally described in terms of its scale of production?
questions.explanation: Garm Hava was produced as a low-budget independent film, relying on realism rather than commercial spectacle.

questions.q: Who composed the music and score for Garm Hava, drawing on a classical and qawwali-influenced sound?
questions.explanation: The sarod maestro Ustad Bahadur Khan composed the music for Garm Hava.

questions.q: The assassination of which national leader is referenced in Garm Hava and forms part of the grim backdrop against which the Mirza family's story unfolds?
questions.explanation: The assassination of Mahatma Gandhi in 1948 is referenced in Garm Hava's imagery, and the months after it set the film's anxious mood; Salim Mirza clings to the hope that Gandhi's martyrdom will not be in vain.

questions.q: Which actor, who later became known as a parallel-cinema and character actor, played Salim Mirza's son Sikandar in Garm Hava?
questions.explanation: Farooq Shaikh played the educated, idealistic son Sikandar in Garm Hava, marking his film debut.

questions.q: The title Garm Hava translates from Urdu into English most closely as which of the following?
questions.explanation: Garm Hava literally means 'hot winds' or 'scorching winds', evoking the harsh climate of post-Partition uncertainty.

questions.q: In the closing of Garm Hava, the son Sikandar decides to join which kind of gathering rather than leave for Pakistan?
questions.explanation: At the end of Garm Hava, Sikandar joins a procession of workers and protesters, signalling hope of belonging through collective struggle.

questions.q: A central recurring hardship for Salim Mirza in Garm Hava is his repeated difficulty in obtaining what from banks and officials?
questions.explanation: Salim Mirza is repeatedly denied loans and credit as banks grow wary of lending to a Muslim family suspected of leaving for Pakistan.

questions.q: Garm Hava was restored and given a fresh theatrical re-release in India roughly four decades after its original release. In which year did this restored re-release occur?
questions.explanation: A digitally restored version of Garm Hava was given a theatrical re-release in India in 2014, around the film's fortieth anniversary.

questions.q: Garm Hava is frequently cited as a pioneering film for portraying Partition from which particular vantage point?
options: The viewpoint of British colonial officers
The perspective of Punjabi refugees crossing the border
The experience of a Muslim family that chose to stay in India
questions.explanation: Garm Hava was a landmark for centring Partition on the dilemma of an ordinary Muslim family that decides to remain in India rather than migrate.
